original_hypothesis.title: NukeFund claim cap can be bypassed by parameter combination (nukeFactorMaxParam vs maxAllowedClaimDivisor) leading to >50% fund claims
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In NukeFund.nuke, claimAmount is only capped to fund/maxAllowedClaimDivisor when finalNukeFactor > nukeFactorMaxParam. If finalNukeFactor is high (e.g., 60,000–99,999) but nukeFactorMaxParam is configured higher than it, the cap does not apply and potentialClaimAmount can exceed the intended maxAllowedClaimAmount (e.g., >50% when divisor=2). A patient holder can then drain a disproportionate share in one nuke.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a dangerous invariant mismatch: cap logic should be min(potentialClaim, maxClaim) regardless of thresholds. Exploitability depends on configured params and achievable finalNukeFactor ranges.
deployment_assumptions: Owner sets nukeFactorMaxParam above maxAllowedClaimAmount thresholding intent (e.g., > MAX_DENOMINATOR/2)
ageMultiplier/defaultNukeFactorIncrease allow finalNukeFactor to reach high values below nukeFactorMaxParam
Fund is sufficiently large to make the drain meaningful
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: NukeFund.sol exposes setNukeFactorMaxParam(uint256) and setMaxAllowedClaimDivisor(uint256) with no bounds checks; cap condition is `finalNukeFactor > nukeFactorMaxParam`

proof_of_signal.pattern: claimAmount = finalNukeFactor > nukeFactorMaxParam ? maxAllowedClaimAmount : potentialClaimAmount; (not min())

preconditions: nukeFactorMaxParam configured such that finalNukeFactor can be high but not exceed it
Attacker holds a token with high entropy/performanceFactor and sufficient age (or configured ageMultiplier) to reach target finalNukeFactor
Attacker can satisfy minimumDaysHeld and approval requirements

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Cap is conditional on factor threshold rather than unconditional minimum-of-two-values
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Yes (parameterization), but exploitation is on-chain once configured
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After parameters are set and tokens mature

operational_questions: What are realistic ranges of finalNukeFactor over time with expected ageMultiplier/defaultNukeFactorIncrease?
Are there operational runbooks preventing unsafe nukeFactorMaxParam values?
Can finalNukeFactor exceed 100000, and if so how quickly?
validation_requirements: Parameter sweep simulation to find configurations where potentialClaim > fund/maxAllowedClaimDivisor without triggering cap
On-chain test with synthetic entropy/age values (fork or local)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Monitor/configure for a high nukeFactorMaxParam regime
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Accumulate/forge for high-entropy tokens and age them to maximize finalNukeFactor
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Execute nuke when finalNukeFactor is high but below nukeFactorMaxParam to claim outsized fund share
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Low once configuration is in place

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Rapid fund depletion beyond advertised cap; unfair payouts and solvency pressure
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High; ETH is paid out irreversibly
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: NukeFund solvency and user trust in payout caps

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Token maturity window; depends on config changes

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Nukes paying > fund/2 (or > configured cap) when divisor=2
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Single-token claims draining more than expected
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Long-held high-entropy tokens nuked immediately after parameter updates

validation_result.mechanism: conditional cap bypass via parameter mismatch
validation_result.impact_summary: The nuke claim cap uses an indirect comparison (finalNukeFactor > nukeFactorMaxParam) instead of a direct min(potentialClaimAmount, maxAllowedClaimAmount). When the two independently configurable parameters fall out of alignment, claims can exceed the intended maximum percentage of the fund.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The nuke() function in NukeFund.sol applies a claim cap only when finalNukeFactor exceeds nukeFactorMaxParam. The intended behavior is that no single nuke should claim more than fund/maxAllowedClaimDivisor (50% with default divisor=2). However, the code uses a proxy comparison on the nuke factor value instead of directly comparing claim amounts. This means the cap is only equivalent to min(potential, max) when nukeFactorMaxParam == MAX_DENOMINATOR / maxAllowedClaimDivisor. Since both parameters can be independently set via setNukeFactorMaxParam() and setMaxAllowedClaimDivisor() with no bounds checks or cross-validation, any configuration change that breaks this relationship creates a window where potentialClaimAmount can exceed maxAllowedClaimAmount without the cap triggering. The default values happen to be aligned (50000 == 100000/2), but this alignment is coincidental and fragile. Additionally, the calculateNukeFactor() function returns an unbounded value that grows with token age, meaning it can exceed MAX_DENOMINATOR entirely. If nukeFactorMaxParam is set above MAX_DENOMINATOR, potentialClaimAmount could exceed the entire fund balance, causing an underflow revert or excessive drain.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: NukeFund.sol nuke() function lines show the conditional cap pattern. Lines for setNukeFactorMaxParam and setMaxAllowedClaimDivisor confirm no bounds checks. The calculateNukeFactor function confirms unbounded growth of finalNukeFactor over time.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The conditional cap compares finalNukeFactor against nukeFactorMaxParam instead of comparing potentialClaimAmount against maxAllowedClaimAmount. When nukeFactorMaxParam > MAX_DENOMINATOR / maxAllowedClaimDivisor, potentialClaimAmount can exceed maxAllowedClaimAmount without the cap engaging. The correct logic should be: claimAmount = potentialClaimAmount > maxAllowedClaimAmount ? maxAllowedClaimAmount : potentialClaimAmount
No bounds check, no validation against maxAllowedClaimDivisor. Allows setting values that break the invariant relationship needed for the conditional cap to work correctly.

No bounds check, no validation against nukeFactorMaxParam. Allows setting values that break the invariant relationship. Setting to a large value (e.g., 10) would mean maxAllowedClaimAmount = fund/10, but nukeFactorMaxParam = 50000 still allows potentialClaimAmount up to 50% of fund.

finalNukeFactor is unbounded - it grows linearly with token age. It can exceed MAX_DENOMINATOR (100000), meaning potentialClaimAmount could exceed the entire fund balance. No upper bound is enforced on the return value.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls nuke(tokenId) with a high-entropy, aged token
5. Conditional: if finalNukeFactor > nukeFactorMaxParam → cap; else → potentialClaimAmount
6. If nukeFactorMaxParam was set higher than MAX_DENOMINATOR/maxAllowedClaimDivisor, the cap doesn't trigger even when potentialClaimAmount > maxAllowedClaimAmount
7. fund -= claimAmount (potentially exceeding the intended cap)
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The vulnerability path requires: (a) nukeFactorMaxParam > MAX_DENOMINATOR/maxAllowedClaimDivisor due to admin parameter changes, and (b) a token with sufficient age and entropy to produce a finalNukeFactor between MAX_DENOMINATOR/maxAllowedClaimDivisor and nukeFactorMaxParam. Example: admin sets nukeFactorMaxParam=80000 while maxAllowedClaimDivisor=2. A token with finalNukeFactor=70000 would claim 70% of fund instead of the intended 50% cap. Even with default nukeFactorMaxParam=50000, raising maxAllowedClaimDivisor to 4 means the intended cap is 25% but any finalNukeFactor between 25000-50000 would claim 25-50% uncapped.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Owner sets nukeFactorMaxParam above maxAllowedClaimAmount thresholding intent
ageMultiplier/defaultNukeFactorIncrease allow finalNukeFactor to reach high values below nukeFactorMaxParam
Fund is sufficiently large to make the drain meaningful
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment script (deployTasks.ts) does not call setNukeFactorMaxParam, setMaxAllowedClaimDivisor, or setAgeMultplier. Defaults are used: nukeFactorMaxParam=50000, maxAllowedClaimDivisor=2, ageMultiplier=0. With ageMultiplier=0, calculateAge always returns 0, making finalNukeFactor = entropy/40 (max ~24999), which cannot exceed 50000. However, ageMultiplier MUST be set for the system to function as designed, and no setter call is in the deployment script.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: With default deployment: ageMultiplier=0 means finalNukeFactor is capped at ~24999 (entropy/40), so potentialClaimAmount max is ~25% of fund. The cap at 50000 never triggers and no bypass occurs. HOWEVER: (1) ageMultiplier must eventually be set for the game to work - once set, finalNukeFactor grows unbounded with time; (2) if admin later changes maxAllowedClaimDivisor to restrict claims further (e.g., to 4 for a 25% cap) without adjusting nukeFactorMaxParam, the gap opens immediately; (3) if nukeFactorMaxParam is raised above 50000 for any reason, the gap opens. The code defect exists in the conditional logic regardless of current configuration.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is HIGH when parameter misalignment occurs: a single nuke operation can drain more than the intended maximum percentage of the fund. With nukeFactorMaxParam=80000 and maxAllowedClaimDivisor=2, up to 80% of the fund can be claimed in one nuke instead of the intended 50%. With nukeFactorMaxParam above MAX_DENOMINATOR, the entire fund (and more, causing revert) could be attempted. The ETH payout is irreversible.
recommended_mitigations: Replace the conditional cap with an unconditional min: claimAmount = potentialClaimAmount > maxAllowedClaimAmount ? maxAllowedClaimAmount : potentialClaimAmount
Add bounds validation in setNukeFactorMaxParam: require(value <= MAX_DENOMINATOR / maxAllowedClaimDivisor)
Add bounds validation in setMaxAllowedClaimDivisor: require(value > 0 && MAX_DENOMINATOR / maxAllowedClaimDivisor >= nukeFactorMaxParam)
Cap finalNukeFactor to MAX_DENOMINATOR in calculateNukeFactor to prevent unbounded growth
Set ageMultiplier in the deployment script to prevent the zero-age issue
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy full protocol with default params. 2. Call setAgeMultplier(1) to enable age-based nuke factor. 3. Call setMaxAllowedClaimDivisor(4) to set 25% cap intent. 4. Leave nukeFactorMaxParam at default 50000. 5. Mint a high-entropy token (entropy ~999999). 6. Wait sufficient time for finalNukeFactor to reach ~40000. 7. Execute nuke - observe claim of 40% of fund instead of intended 25%. Alternative: set nukeFactorMaxParam(80000) with divisor=2, age a token to finalNukeFactor=70000, observe 70% claim instead of 50%.
uncertainties: With deployed defaults (ageMultiplier=0), the vulnerability is dormant since finalNukeFactor maxes at ~24999
No evidence in deployment scripts of intended parameter changes post-deployment
The system is fundamentally broken without setting ageMultiplier, suggesting incomplete deployment
Whether admin would ever set parameters that break alignment is speculative but the code should be defensive
additional_testing_needed: Fuzz test with randomized nukeFactorMaxParam and maxAllowedClaimDivisor combinations to find parameter spaces where claimAmount exceeds fund/maxAllowedClaimDivisor
Long-duration simulation to determine when finalNukeFactor exceeds nukeFactorMaxParam with various ageMultiplier settings
Test with finalNukeFactor > MAX_DENOMINATOR to verify overflow/underflow behavior
Integration test verifying cap invariant holds across all parameter setter permutations
